title: Veterinary (Licensing of Veterinary Laboratory Technicians) Regulations, 2011
title_en: Veterinary (Licensing of Veterinary Laboratory Technicians) Regulations, 2011

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Veterinary (Licensing of Veterinary Laboratory Technicians) Regulations, 2011.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Veterinary Act; [Cap. 279] “ License ” means a certificate issued to a laboratory technician under these regulations; “ Licensing Authority ” means the Veterinary Council of Tamzania; “ Laboratory technician ” means a person who hold a diploma in veterinary laboratory; “ Council ” means the Veterinary Council of Tanzania established under section 3 of the Act ; “ Registrar ” means the Registrar of the Council appointed under section 8 of the Act . Part II – Licensing of veterinary laboratory technicians

provisions.title: Qualification for licensing veterinary laboratory technician
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to practice as veterinary laboratory technician shall apply for a license to the Council and he shall be:— (a) a person of good mental and moral character; (b) a person who has received training and awarded a diploma in veterinary laboratory technician or the equivalent qualification recognised by the Council ; (c) a citizen of Tanzania; (d) a person who has not commited any offence or acts specified under the Act . (2) The Minister shall, on the recommendations of the Council , publish in the Gazette qualifications which entitle the holder for licensing in terms of these regulations. (3) A foreigner may be entitled for licensing as a laboratory technician if that foreigner:— (a) intends to undertake a special work for which no Tanzanian is qualified; (b) has passed an examination administered by the council for that purpose; and (c) has paid an application fee of Tanzania shillings equivalent to one hundred US Dollars.

provisions.title: Procedure for licensing
provisions.content: (1) Any person who intends to be licensed as a veterinary laboratory technician shall submit his application in the prescribed form set out in the first schedule and that application shall be accompanied by evidence of qualifications and an application fee of Tanzania shillings five thousand. (2) The Registrar shall, after the receipt of the application under these regulations submit such application to the Council for final determination. (3) The Council may, in determining the application submitted to it, direct the Registrar to licence the applicant upon payment of a licensing fee of Tanzania shillings ten thousand or Tanzania shillings equivalent to two hundred US Dollars in case the applicant is a foreigner.

provisions.title: Retention fees
provisions.content: (1) there shall be a retention fee to be paid annually to the council by the licensed veterinary laboratory technician. (2) The fees ferred under sub-regulation (1) shall be Tanzania shillings five thousand shillings in case of Tanzanian, and one hundred US Dollars in case of foreigners.

provisions.title: List of licensed veterinary laboratory technicians
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall cause to be kept the list of veterinary laboratory technicians to whom the Council has granted license under these regulations. (2) The list kept under sub-regulation (1) shall contain:— (a) physical address of a licensed person; (b) particulars of licensing and date of first commencement; (c) names, qualifications, place where qualifications was obtained, date of obtaining the qualifications and any other personal data, to be determined from time to time by the Council .

provisions.title: Publication of licensed veterinary laboratory technicians
provisions.content: The Minister shall, on the recommendation of the Council and by notice in the Gazette publish names, qualifications and address of licensed veterinary laboratory technicians. Part III – Offences and penalties

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any veterinary laboratory technician who:— (a) make untruthful or deceitful or misleading reports; (b) conduct himself dishonestly or unfairly; or (c) unethically signs or validates a report without actually or physically carrying out the analysis, commits and offence. (2) Any veterinary laboratory technician who gives any false information, or any information which he reasonably believe to be untrue; commits an offence and shall upon conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding Tanzania information five hundred or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both such fine and imprisonment. (3) Any person who contravenes a provision of these Regulations commits an offence and shall upon conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both.
